page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-16 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM. Powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9B14 processor, the instance provides 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The dedicated CPU allocation includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including VNNI and BF16, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25 percent. Text processing and text generation for small language models also demonstrate strong execution speeds. However, multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth fall into the average tier. A notable hardware tradeoff is the uncached memory write bandwidth of 12.89 GB/sec, which ranks in the bottom 10 percent of tested systems.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3d-highcpu-16 offers a dense compute-to-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core, making it a resource-efficient option for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. Appropriate workloads for this instance include software compilation, web serving, text processing, and light machine learning inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance configured with 16 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, utilizing an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with 8 physical cores. The instance features dedicated CPU allocation and supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution and text processing capabilities, alongside average multi-core and memory read speeds. A key tradeoff is its poor uncached memory write bandwidth of 12.89 GB/sec. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for tasks requiring high CPU density relative to memory, such as software compilation, web serving, and text processing.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a balanced resource profile for CPU-intensive workloads.

Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration
